pattern: Inserting a whole level means finding every node one above the target depth and splicing two new nodes between each parent and its original children, with the root case handled specially.
hints: When the target depth is 1 there is no parent level, so the whole existing tree just becomes a child of a brand new root.
Otherwise you want to stop at the nodes sitting at depth minus one and rebuild their links.
At each such parent, the old left child must hang off the new left node and the old right child off the new right node, preserving order.

edge_inputs.description: insert at depth 2 below the root
insert deeper where some branches are missing
depth 1 wraps the whole tree under a new root
single node, insert as its children
skewed left chain, insert at the bottom
negative value inserted in the middle

stress.description: random level-order tree; pick a valid depth within the tree's height plus one
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1500))
    arr = [rng.randint(-100, 100)]
    for _ in range(n - 1):
        arr.append(rng.randint(-100, 100) if rng.random() < 0.7 else None)
    while arr and arr[-1] is None:
        arr.pop()
    # estimate height by simulating level-order parent/child layout
    idx = 0
    children = [i for i, v in enumerate(arr) if v is not None]
    # crude depth bound: number of nodes is enough as an upper bound on depth
    depth = rng.randint(1, max(1, len([v for v in arr if v is not None])))
    val = rng.randint(-100000, 100000)
    return [arr, val, depth]
stress.note: depth bounded by node count keeps it valid for any shape
